items.title: Overriding and dynamic dispatch: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Java: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Every same-named member uses dynamic dispatch, including fields, static methods, overloads, and constructor calls.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Dynamic dispatch applies to overridden instance methods on the runtime receiver; use @Override and distinguish it from overload selection and member hiding.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Dynamic dispatch applies to overridden instance methods on the runtime receiver; use @Override and distinguish it from overload selection and member hiding.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Dynamic dispatch applies to overridden instance methods on the runtime receiver; use @Override and distinguish it from overload selection and member hiding.
Every same-named member uses dynamic dispatch, including fields, static methods, overloads, and constructor calls.

answer.explanation: Dynamic dispatch applies to overridden instance methods on the runtime receiver; use @Override and distinguish it from overload selection and member hiding.

items.title: Overriding and dynamic dispatch: runnable core task
items.prompt: Read dog or cat. Store the chosen subtype in an Animal reference and print the overridden sound selected at runtime.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Start from the stated input/output contract, implement the smallest language-specific operation, and run the visible boundary before changing structure.

feedback.correct: The visible and hidden Java: Beginner to Intermediate runner checks match the declared contract.

answer.explanation: The compile-time reference is Animal, but the overridden instance method is selected from the runtime receiver.
